summary: Frontend-only retest of multi-page expansion (8 service pages, routing, dropdown, related nav, dialogs). All 17 critical flows passed end-to-end against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.

issues: Same a11y/SEO defect as Hero.jsx (iteration_1): ServicePage.jsx WordReveal (lines 17-25) renders each word as adjacent inline-block spans with mr-[0.25em] margin and NO whitespace text node between them. innerText returns concatenated string e.g. 'Brandsthatmeansomething.' / 'Growthyoucanmeasure.' Visual spacing is purely CSS. Affects screen readers, SEO snippets, copy-paste. Fix: insert ' ' between mapped <span> siblings or add aria-label on the h1 with the full sentence (already partly done via aria-label on the outer motion.span, but inner words still use aria-hidden so SR readers may rely on outer label — verify VoiceOver/NVDA pronunciation). Recommend also normalising innerText with whitespace for SEO crawlers.

Timing observation: after hover-dropdown click on dropdown-performance-ads and waiting 500ms post wait_for_url, document.title was still the default 'Vibify — Make Brands Impossible to Ignore' (not 'Performance Ads — Vibify'). Direct goto to /services/performance-ads sets the correct title. Likely cause: AnimatePresence mode='wait' delays new-page mount until old-page exit (~350ms) + framer transition before useEffect (which sets document.title) runs. Not a functional bug — title resolves shortly after — but a quick check (waiting longer or moving title set into a top-level effect) would make SPA title transitions snappier and avoid the stale-title flash in the browser tab during the cross-fade.

action_items: Fix WordReveal whitespace (insert ' ' between word spans) in /app/frontend/src/pages/ServicePage.jsx (and ideally share with /app/frontend/src/components/landing/Hero.jsx) for a11y/SEO.

Optional polish: ensure document.title updates fire even during framer-motion AnimatePresence transitions (e.g., set title in a useLayoutEffect or in the route element wrapper, not after exit animation completes).
critical_code_review_comments: ServicePage.jsx is well-structured (~300 lines, single responsibility). Data lives in services.js — clean separation.
ServicePage re-exports `services` (export { services }) which is unusual and could create circular-import risk; importing directly from data/services.js is cleaner.
WordReveal is duplicated logic between Hero.jsx and ServicePage.jsx with the same whitespace bug — extract into a shared component /components/landing/WordReveal.jsx to fix once.
service-page-{slug} testid on root is good. Showcase, features, process, stats, related all have stable testids. Nice work.
No unique partial index on (type,email) for newsletter — pre-existing race condition flagged in iteration_1, still applies.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (17/17 flows: homepage cards, 8-route direct nav, all section testids, dropdown 8 items, related nav, logo home, invalid-email validation, contact submit POST /api/contact, booking submit POST /api/booking, /admin still lists submissions)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ContactSubmission (TEST_service_1782376380@example.com) and TEST_BookingSubmission (TEST_booking_1782376381@example.com) via dialog submits. Visible in /admin (Total now 13). Not auto-cleaned — safe to leave (prefix TEST_).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://vibify-bold.preview.emergentagent.com. Services dropdown opens on HOVER (mouseenter) — use page.hover before click. After SPA route change allow 400–600ms for framer AnimatePresence mode='wait' transition before asserting testids; for document.title via dropdown, allow ~800ms to be safe. CountUp animates so service-stat-* text may show intermediate values (e.g. '127+' before reaching '250+'); assert presence/visibility, not exact number, unless waiting ~2s. ContactDialog is shared for both modes — same testids contact-name-input / contact-email-input / contact-message-input / contact-submit-button / contact-success / field-error work for service-start-cta (contact) AND service-book-cta (booking).
rca of the issue: No new bugs reproduced. Pre-existing WordReveal whitespace issue replicated in ServicePage.jsx — RCA identical to iteration_1 Hero.jsx: each word rendered as {word} with no whitespace text node between siblings; visual spacing comes from CSS margin (mr-[0.25em]). Mitigation: extract into shared WordReveal component and emit ' ' between word spans, or set whitespace-pre on parent and include real spaces in the mapped source.
